description: Migration: create inbound_email_rules table with (tenant, id) composite PK, position/provider_ids/conditions/action_type/action_config/on_no_match/fallback_inbound_ticket_defaults_id columns, tenant+position index, Citus distribution, transaction:false
Migration: create client_name_aliases table with (tenant, id) PK, client_id, alias, unique index on (tenant, lower(alias)), tenant+client index, Citus distribution
Migration: extend email_processed_messages processing_status check constraint to include 'skipped'
Shared TypeScript types for InboundEmailRule, RuleCondition, per-action-type action_config shapes, and evaluation outcome
Validation schemas for rule create/update payloads: condition field/operator enums, action_config shape per action_type, on_no_match enum, regex pattern length cap
Evaluator: case-insensitive equals/contains/starts_with/ends_with operators over condition fields
Evaluator: matches_regex operator with try/catch compile (invalid = condition false, logged once per rule per process) and pattern length cap
Evaluator: field accessors from parsed email — from_address, from_domain, to_address (any recipient), subject, body_text sliced to ~100KB
Extractor: between/after/before templates with occurrence first/last, compiled to the same internal extractor as raw regex
Extractor: raw regex extraction using capture group 1
Extracted-value normalization: trim, collapse internal whitespace, lowercase; empty result = non-match
Client matcher: normalized exact match against clients.client_name, excluding inactive clients
Client matcher: fallback to client_name_aliases lookup (tenant-scoped, case-insensitive)
Rule loader: single indexed query for active tenant rules ordered by position, filtered in-process by provider_ids (NULL = all)
Evaluation loop: first conditions-match executes action; resolved action stops evaluation; on_no_match=proceed continues to subsequent rules
on_no_match=skip outcome: email skipped when the matched rule's extraction/classification fails
on_no_match=fallback_destination outcome: ticket created using fallback_inbound_ticket_defaults_id
Engine error isolation: any unexpected evaluator error logs a warning with rule id and falls through to the unmodified pipeline
Pipeline hook in processInboundEmailInApp: rules evaluated on new-ticket path only, after thread matching fails, before resolveInboundTicketDefaults and sender matching
Skip action: no ticket/comment/attachment processing; email_processed_messages row with processing_status='skipped' and metadata { ruleId, ruleName }
Skip action works for tenants with no inbound_ticket_defaults configured (evaluated before defaults requirement)
Rule-assigned client takes precedence over exact sender-contact match and domain match
Contact attribution for rule-assigned client: sender contact within that client if present, else the client's primary contact
Rule-assigned client flows through resolveEffectiveInboundTicketDefaults so client-level defaults overrides apply
set_destination action: referenced inbound_ticket_defaults applied at the top of the destination cascade; sender matching still attributes client/contact
Created tickets record appliedRuleId and clientMatchSource in tickets.email_metadata
INBOUND_EMAIL_RECEIVED activity-log entry includes the applied rule name
One structured log line per evaluated email: rules considered, rule matched, outcome
Dangling reference handling: deleted fallback/destination defaults set or deleted/inactive aliased client degrades to non-match/proceed with warning
AI classify OSS stub: dynamic loader returns no_decision (inboundReplyAcknowledgementDecider pattern)
AI classify EE module: builds prompt from instruction + subject/from/body excerpt; no tenant client list in prompt
AI classify EE module: constrained JSON response parsing { decision: skip|assign_client|no_decision, extracted_client_name }
AI assign_client decision resolves extracted_client_name through the same exact+alias client matcher
allowed_outcomes enforcement: AI decisions outside the rule's allowed_outcomes are treated as no_decision
AI failure handling: timeout/error/missing add-on/malformed response = non-match routed to on_no_match; ticket creation never blocks on AI
AI token usage logged through the AI module's existing usage tracking
Server action: list inbound email rules for tenant
Server action: create rule with full payload validation
Server action: update rule with full payload validation
Server action: delete rule
Server action: reorder rules (persist positions atomically)

Server action: test rule — runs the shared evaluator against a draft rule + sample From/Subject/Body without persisting; returns per-condition pass/fail, extracted value, resolved client, final outcome
Server actions: client name alias CRUD (list per client, create, delete) with uniqueness error surfaced
RBAC: all rule/alias/test actions require the email-provider-admin permission
UI: Inbound Rules section in email settings alongside provider list and InboundTicketDefaultsManager
UI: rules table with name, human-readable summary, mailbox-filter chips, active toggle, edit/delete
UI: drag-to-reorder rules persisting position via reorder action
UI: human-readable rule summary generator (e.g. "From contains @huntress.com → assign client from subject")
UI: inline active/inactive toggle on rule rows
UI: rule editor drawer with name, active toggle, mailbox multi-select (empty = all mailboxes)
UI: condition builder — add/remove rows of field + operator + value; matches-regex available as a normal operator
UI: action picker for the four action types
UI: AI action visible but disabled with upsell hint when EE + AI add-on is not active
UI: extraction config — source select, template select (between/after/before/regex), delimiter/pattern inputs, occurrence select
UI: destination picker backed by active inbound_ticket_defaults sets (for set_destination and fallback)
UI: AI action config — instruction textarea and allowed-outcomes checkboxes
UI: non-match behavior select; fallback destination picker shown only when fallback_destination selected
UI: validation errors from server schemas surfaced inline in the editor
UI: live tester panel — sample From/Subject/Body inputs invoking the test-rule action
UI: tester results — per-condition pass/fail, extracted value, resolved client or no-match, final outcome
UI: tester alias quick-add — when extraction succeeds but no client resolves, offer adding the value as an alias with a client picker
UI: "Matching aliases" section on the client record next to inbound email domains, with add/remove
